objects.name: NewText
objects.string: Oi! Me chamo Myla e sou aluna do 1º ano. Este jogo foi o primeiro que desenvolvi sozinha. Utilizei as artes do pacote ''Sprout Lands'' criado para desenvolvedores de jogos. O intuito do meu trabalho visa conscientizar sobre como nossas atitudes afetam o meio ambiente. Espero que goste! ;)

content.text: Oi! Me chamo Myla e sou aluna do 1º ano. Este jogo foi o primeiro que desenvolvi sozinha. Utilizei as artes do pacote ''Sprout Lands'' criado para desenvolvedores de jogos. O intuito do meu trabalho visa conscientizar sobre como nossas atitudes afetam o meio ambiente. Espero que goste! ;)

objects.name: NewText2
objects.string: Que saco! Os humanos deixaram minha fazenda suja de novo. Melhor eu arrumar isso recolhendo o lixo!

content.text: Que saco! Os humanos deixaram minha fazenda suja de novo. Melhor eu arrumar isso recolhendo o lixo!

objects.name: NewText3
objects.string: Peguei o item errado! Tenho que recolher o lixo apenas.

content.text: Peguei o item errado! Tenho que recolher o lixo apenas.

objects.name: NewText5
objects.string: Finalmente limpei tudo! Agora posso descansar.

content.text: Finalmente limpei tudo! Agora posso descansar.

objects.name: NewText6
objects.string: Preciso pegar esse lixo espalhado pela fazenda!

content.text: Preciso pegar esse lixo espalhado pela fazenda!

objects.name: NewText7
objects.string: Peguei o certo! Esse lixo polui o meio ambiente e os animais podem comer pensando ser comida.

content.text: Peguei o certo! Esse lixo polui o meio ambiente e os animais podem comer pensando ser comida.

eventsFunctionsExtensions.author: @4ian
eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: StayOnScreen
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: Constrain object position to remain within the camera viewport boundaries.
description: Force the object to stay visible on the screen by setting back its 2D position (X and Y coordinates) inside the viewport of the camera.
The shoot'em up example uses this extension ([open the project online](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?project=example://space-shooter)).

eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: SpriteMultitouchJoystick
description: Multitouch joysticks are objects showing a joystick on the screen, useful for mobile. They work like a physical gamepad:

- Automatic "mapper" behaviors for 2D and 3D movement behaviors (platformer characters, top-down movement, 3D character etc...). Add one of these to the object with the movement behavior (i.e: the player most of the time) and the behavior will then be controlled automatically by the virtual joystick (it works by reading the multitouch joystick state and simulating controls). No need for additional events to make it work once the behaviors are set up.
There are ready-to-use joysticks in the asset-store [multitouch joysticks pack](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?initial-dialog=asset-store&asset-pack=multitouch-joysticks-multitouch-joysticks).

eventsFunctionsExtensions.author: Bouh
eventsFunctionsExtensions.name: Gamepads
eventsFunctionsExtensions.shortDescription: Gamepad/controller support: buttons, sticks, triggers, vibration. Mapper behaviors for 2D/3D.
description: Add support for gamepads (or other physical controllers).

- automatic "gamepad mapper" behaviors for 2D and 3D movement behaviors (platformer characters, top-down movement, 3D character etc...). Add one of these to the object with the movement behavior (i.e: the player most of the time) and the behavior will then be controlled automatically by the gamepad (it works by reading the gamepad state and simulating controls). No need for additional events to make it work once the behaviors are set up.
The Bomberman-like example handles 4 players with gamepads ([open the project online](https://editor.gdevelop.io/?project=example://goose-bomberman)).

eventsFunctions.description:
Test if a button is released on a gamepad. Buttons can be:
* Xbox: "A", "B", "X", "Y", "LB", "RB", "LT", "RT", "BACK", "START",
* PS4: "CROSS", "SQUARE", "CIRCLE", "TRIANGLE", "L1", "L2", "R1", "R2", "SHARE", "OPTIONS", "PS_BUTTON", "CLICK_TOUCHPAD",
* Other: "UP", "DOWN", "LEFT", "RIGHT", "CLICK_STICK_LEFT", "CLICK_STICK_RIGHT".

* Other: "UP", "DOWN", "LEFT", "RIGHT", "CLICK_STICK_LEFT", "CLICK_STICK_RIGHT".
